entry.title: New Mass-Upload of Test Cases in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
New mass upload of test cases in SAP Cloud ALM

It is now possible to upload a large number of test cases to SAP Cloud ALM using the mass upload feature.
With this blog post I briefly describe the process.

1. Call the Test Preparation app.

2. Navigate to the "Upload Button"

3. Download the template to fill it with your test cases

4. Perform the upload

Ideas and input for SAP

  • What would certainly be helpful is downloading existing test cases in SAP Cloud ALM, editing them in Excel, and uploading them again.
  • The exchange and reuse of test cases between multiple SAP Cloud ALM projects would also be helpful.
  • SAP should also consider the easy storage of test cases from multiple projects into a central operational project. This is a requirement I hear from many customers.

Conclusion

In any case, the new mass upload of test cases is a very helpful feature and a first big step in the right direction.

It saves us a lot of time and makes test management much more efficient.

entry.title: Re: First consolidation_rule based
entry.content:
Hi,

Assuming that you use the delivered content, using a different sub item will still trigger the elimination of the investments because the reclassification rules (010-020) include all sub items (the filter is based on the C/I activity 01), but this might be reflected differently in the cash flow statement because the Investments FS item is assigned to different reporting items (SCF421-SCF423...) depending on the sub items.

See also in the note https://me.sap.com/notes/2659656/E the attachment Scenarios for Rule-Based Consolidation of Investments.pdf. The first consolidation scenario is described and mentions explicitely the sub item 920.

Hope this helps.

Grégoire

entry.title: Re: Facing issue of email alerts are not able to send.
entry.content: We are facing issue of email alerts are not able to send. We have done the following. - SLD alert setting is enabled - All SAP services are running - Restarted Job services & Service layer - Alert Query is showing result -Alert user id unlocked & have super user rights.

entry.title: Operation Subcontracting in Production Order
entry.content:
I have a scenario where there is a routing with five operations (0010, 0020, 0030, 0040, and 0050), out of which the second operation 0020 is performed externally. We issue material X1 to first operation 0010 and material X2 to second operation 0020 on subcontractor end. Then rest operations (i.e. 0030, 0040, and 0050) are performed in house once we receive semi-finished material SFG2 from subcontractor end.

So in this scenario I have few questions for operation subcontracting.

  1. You issue first component X1 to first operation and then your first operation produce a semi-finished material (Let’s assume it is SFG1). Now you provide second component X2 and SFG1 to subcontractor. So my question is, where stock of SFG1 will come from in system? 
  2. If I consider SFG1 is a dummy material or non-valuated material then also how its stock will come in system so that I can provide to subcontractor. (Is stock uploading mov 561 recommended)?  
  3. If I try to receive stock of SFG1 as a by-product (mov 531), then I have to receive it at the time of first operation confirmation (Where in routing I have used PP01 control key), so how can I receive because by-products are generally received at the time of GR against production order (Last operation confirmation with control key PP03)?

Thanks in advance for any quick insights!

entry.title: Re: Monitoring of automatic triggers (subscriptions) in S...
entry.content:
Hello Ankit Gupta,

in case you Subscription triggers a Production Process you can use the Monitor Production Process app as already mentioned by you.

If you would like to have specifically logs for the subscription you can go to Configure Production Connectivity app, select Shop Floor System tab and select the Shop Floor System configured in the Subscription configuration. Alternatively you can navigate to details from Subscription configuration as well:

On this Screen select the Agent Instance according to the set Retention Time in the Subscription and open the Logs: 

In the logs you will find the necessary details:

I hope this helps !

Regards,

Marcel Wolf

entry.title: Advanced Shipping Notification needs to be triggered once the Freight Lists.
entry.content:
Our business requirement is to trigger ASN when we release the freight list. Is there any solution?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Shipping Notification needs to be triggered...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The advanced shipping notification (ASN) will be sent to your customer once the relevant outbound delivery document is released. The ASN will be send either by XML (Extensible Markup Language) or Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) based on the settings you have scoped. Processing of freight list does not have any impact on the ASN being triggered in Business ByDesign.

Regards, 
Anand
SAP Technical Support. 

Note : Please refer the help document "Standard Outbound Delivery Processing for Sales Orders — Standard Shipping" which explains the same in detail.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Just Ask機能のご紹介
entry.content:
はじめに

本ブログでは、 QRC 2024 Q1 or 2024.02 にてリリースされた、SAP Analytics CloudのJust Ask機能についてご紹介します。本ブログはInitial Release of SAP Analytics Cloud Just Askを参考に作成しています。

Just Askは、AIを活用した自然言語クエリ機能です。Just Ask を使用すると、ユーザーは自然言語で質問することで回答をチャートやテーブルで得ることができます。※2025年4月現在、英語のみの対応です。

SAP Analytics CloudのインポートモデルとSAP Datasphereのモデルをサポートしています。
Just Askは、Amazon Web Services 、Google Cloud PlatformおよびMicrosoft Azureでホストされているテナントで利用できます。

本ブログの内容

  • Just Askへのアクセス方法
  • Just Askの基本機能
  • Just Askの利用イメージ
  • 管理機能概要
  • ロールアウトの準備

Just Askへのアクセス方法
Just Askは、ホーム画面の検索バーまたはメインツールバーの電球アイコンから利用することが可能です。

ホーム画面の検索バーの表示/非表示はオプションであり、ホーム画面の設定から有効にすることができます。

Just Askメイン画面

 

Just Askの基本機能

モデルの選択
質問する前に、クエリ対象のモデルを選択して絞り込みます。モデルを選択しないで利用することも可能ですが、検索精度向上のために対象モデルを選択する事が推奨されます。使用可能なモデルは、管理者によってJust Askに追加され、インデックス付けされたインポートデータモデルです。

SAP Datasphereのモデルを利用するには、 [検索に別のモデルを追加] を選択してデータモデルを選択します。SAP Datasphere の接続とスペースを参照し、SAP Datasphere 分析モデルまたは分析データセットを選択します。選択すると、SAP Datasphere モデルはセッションの期間中は使用可能になります。

 

質問を入力またはシステム提案の利用
検索バーに質問を入力します。入力中は入力候補が灰色で表示され、右矢印キーを押すことで単語の自動補完に使用できます。マウスまたはキーボードを使用して、ドロップダウンから他の候補を選択することもできます。

また検索後には、画面の右側に前の質問に基づいて提案された質問が表示されます。

メジャーとディメンションピッカー
検索バーの左側にある 「+」アイコンを押すと、モデルのメジャー（数値項目）とディメンション（分析軸）を直接選択し、フィルタすることができます。

サンプル質問
入力補助提案に加えて、管理者はサンプルの質問を定義できます。サンプル質問は、Just Askのメイン画面から利用できます。

 

Just Askの利用イメージ
Just Askは自然言語で質問するだけで分析クエリを生成し、チャートによるビジュアライゼーションで回答します。ビジュアライゼーションの上部には、クエリから推測されたフィルタが表示されます。この例では、「show gross margin by store in 2016」という質問に対して、年月を2016年に絞るフィルタを生成しています。

画面上部のボタンを使用して、チャートの種類を切り替えや表形式での表示、軸の追加やフィルタの追加、ドリルダウン操作などを行うことが可能であり、分析を深めることが可能です。また、データアナライザを開いてさらに分析したり、最適化されたストーリーに貼り付けることができるクリップボードにコピーしたりできます (従来のストーリーエクスペリエンスはサポートされていません)。

※データアナライザ＝ピヴォットライクに分析対象の数値項目や分析軸を切り替えながらデータ探索・分析を行える機能

また、Just Askの回答の画面の「…」メニューからソートを行ったり、データをCSVでエクスポートすることも可能です、

 

管理機能概要
Just Ask管理者は、Just Ask管理権限を持つロールが割り当てられたアカウントです。管理者、BI 管理者、BI コンテンツ作成者、モデラーなどの事前定義済みの組み込みロールには、この権限が含まれています。

 

管理者のタスク
Just Ask管理者の主なタスクは、エンドユーザーがクエリできるようにモデルを追加することです。

「モデルを管理」ボタンからモデル管理画面にアクセスします。

Just Askモデル管理画面 

追加されたモデルはインデックスが作成され、管理者は同義語とルールを定義できます。詳細については、後述のロールアウトセクションを参照してください。 

インデックスが作成されたモデルとインデックスがないモデル
Just Askはインデックスが作成されたモデルとインデックスがないモデルの両方をクエリできます。以下の表に違いをまとめます。

 

インデックスモデル

インデックスなし

管理者がモデルを照会する必要がない

管理者が同義語とルールを定義可能

初期NLQスコープ

取得した（インポート）モデル

SAP Datasphere モデル

ユーザーによるモデル選択が必要

エンドユーザーは選択したモデルにクエリを絞り込むことができる

 

「Just Ask」をオンにする
デフォルトではJust Ask は有効化されておらず、Search to Insight 機能がデフォルトになっています。管理者は「システム管理」ページの「デフォルトの外観」タブにあるテナント全体の会話分析のトグルをオンにしてJust Ask を有効にします。
QRC 2025Q1より、Just Askはデフォルトで有効化されています。

 

SAP Datasphere
エンドユーザーがSAP Datasphere分析モデルと分析データセットを選択するには、SAP Datasphere接続を作成する必要があります。エンドユーザーには、接続:読み取り権限を持つSAP Analytics Cloudロールが割り当てられている必要があります。エンドユーザーは、SAP Datasphereユーザーアカウントを持ち、少なくとも1つのスペースのメンバーであり、モデルを読み取る権限を持っている必要があります。

詳細については、次のドキュメントを参照してください:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/ad4281e2875949f0b4d45...

 

ロールアウトの準備

データモデリング
Just Askを有効にする前に、管理者は、データモデルをJust Askに追加する必要があります。場合によっては、ストーリー作成向けに設計された既存のデータモデルがJust Askでのユーザーの要件に適さない場合があり、Just Askでの使用に特化したモデリング作業が必要になる場合があります。たとえば、モデルで公開されるエンティティが多すぎる場合や、専門知識を必要とする意味を持つエンティティがある場合、モデルにユーザーが期待するメジャーや計算が含まれていない場合などです。

 

同義語
モデリング作業を補完し、エンドユーザーが使用し慣れている用語に合わせてカスタマイズするために、管理者はモデルエンティティとディメンション名の同義語を定義できます。

ディメンション名の同義語の定義

ディメンション名に同義語を定義します。

 

ルール
エンドユーザーの質問に、質問に含まれていない暗黙のコンテキストが含まれている場合、ルールを使用して適切な回答を提供できます。たとえば、ユーザーは顧客訪問と旅費の関係について「顧客訪問と旅費を表示してください」と質問しますが、探しているのは「営業担当者による顧客訪問と旅費を表示してください」というような状況です。

ルール条件はJust Askに対する質問で使用される用語に基づいており、条件が満たされると、結果の並べ替え、フィルターの追加、エンティティの削除などのアクションが実行されます。

 

権限管理
データモデルは、その同義語とルールとともに、特定のユーザーを対象としています。特定のモデルにアクセスできるのは対象ユーザーのみに制限するために細心の注意を払う必要があります。Just Askの管理画面で必要なモデルだけを追加するまたは不要なものを除外することや、SAP Analytics Cloud と SAP Datasphere でモデルアクセス権が適切に定義されていることを確認することが望ましいです。

ロールアウト
対象ユーザーと対象データモデルごとに段階的なアプローチで公開することが可能です。現在、Just Askではモデルのメタデータ情報を検索することはできないため上記の手順に加えて、ユーザーがデータモデルを理解できるようにトレーニングや資料作成を行うのが望ましい場合もあります。

本ブログではSAP Analytics Cloud Just Ask機能の概要をご紹介しました。

entry.title: Dangerous Goods Information (3G8) for Warehouse documents
entry.content:
Hello,

we activated the scope item 3G8 (Dangerous Goods in the Value Chain). This scope item is used to print dangerous goods designations on documents such as delivery note and delivery pickling list (MM).

Because we use Warehouse Management, our the pick list is generated from Warehouse Order in WM (EWM Pick List). With scope item 3G8 the dangerous goods information is not displayed on this docuement, but we need it there. 

What is the intended standard process for this case?

I haven't been able to find any released CDS-View for dangerous goods information to use custom logic.

Thank you for your help!

Best regards, 

Frederik

entry.title: How to get BYD Audit Report
entry.content:
Hi SAP BYD Team,

 

Our audits have requested we to provide the following report as part of the audit of our FY24 financials:

SAP Business ByDesign SOC 2 Audit Report

can you advise how to obtain a copy.



Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to get BYD Audit Report
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

To get the audit report, please kindly go to SAP Trust Center (https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center.html). The audit report for cloud product can be achieved in this website.

Please go to Compliance Tab and you will find all the available audit documents provided by SAP. Choose the target report and specify the product and period. And then you will find the necessary report.

If you cannot found the target report or have any issue when requesting the report, please contact your account owner or directly contact trust center team by the "Contact Us" Tab in SAP Trust Center.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Technical Support,

Cariford

entry.title: CAPのHybrid Testingの実行までに有用な疎通テスト：SAP S/4HANA On-Premiseへの接続
entry.content:
はじめに

この記事では、SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)において、SAP S/4HANA On-Premiseの実データにアクセスしながら開発を行うための「Hybrid Testing」について説明します。Hybrid Testing実行までに役立つ疎通テストに焦点を当て、具体的な環境構築方法については触れませんので、そちらの詳細を知りたい方は以下のリンクを参考にしてください。

  • SAP Gateway - Activate and Maintain Services
  • Discovery Center - Connect S/4HANA system using Cloud Connector
  • SAP CAP  - Consuming Services #Connect to Remote Services Locally
  • SAP CAP - Hybrid Testing
  • SAP Cloud SKD - Connecting to External Systems From the Business Application Studio #Connection to an On-Premise System

Hybrid Testingとは？

CAPを用いたアプリ開発において、基本的にはモックデータを使用したローカル環境でテストを行いますが、クラウドやオンプレのサービスにアクセスしたい場合があります。そこで役立つ手法が「Hybrid Testing」です。このテスト方式を使うことで、開発者はローカル環境でアプリを実行しながらリモートサービスに接続できるため、開発効率の向上が見込めます。

SAP S/4HANA On-Premiseに対するHybrid Testingの課題

上記の利点がある一方で、オンプレにアクセスする際にはSAP Destination Service, SAP Connectivity Service, Cloud Connector といったサービスに依存するため、モックデータを使ったローカルテストと比較すると環境の整備が煩雑になります。そこで、環境構築をスムーズに進めるために使える疎通テストを紹介します。

疎通テスト一覧

紹介する疎通テストは下記の図の順番となっています。依存関係が少ないものから書き出していますので、順にテストすることをお勧めします。

1. SAP Gateway Clientからの疎通テスト

T-codeが /IWFND/GW_CLIENT であるSAP Gateway Clientは、OData サービスをテストする HTTP (S)クライアントとして機能します。適切なHTTP メソッド、ODataクエリ(format, top...)、リクエストURI、Content Typeなどのリクエストヘッダー、ペイロードを入力した後、アプリ上の「Execute」をクリックして、S/4HANA内で対象のODataサービスの利用可否やレスポンス結果を確認します。

エラーが発生した際には、ODataサービスが有効化されているか、正しいpathを指定しているかどうかなどをチェックしてください。

2. Cloud Connector administration UIからの疎通テスト

Gateway Clientからのテストが成功した後は、SCCからオンプレへの疎通をテストします。具体的には、SCCで定義したVirtual hostからInternal hostであるオンプレへの接続を確認するために、Actionsの一番左のアイコンをクリックしてください。'Reachable'が表示されたら成功です。

3. BTP Cockpitからの疎通テスト

BTP Cockpit上でSCCに対する「Check Connection」を行うことでDestinationのURLへの到達が可能か確認できます。ただし、ここでのテストはDestination ServiceであるSCCのVirtual hostが稼働していることを保証するものではなく、到達可能かを確認するという点に注意してください。

'The check does not guarantee that the target system or service is operational. It only verifies if a connection is possible.'

エラーが発生した場合、SCCの設定やバージョン、サーバーのアベイラビリティに問題がある可能性があります。

4. SAP Business Application Studio(BAS)からの疎通テスト

最後はHybrid Testingです。Cloud Foundry Runtimeにデプロイしたアプリに対してdestinationやauthサービスをバインドし、下記コマンドを実行した状態でHTTPファイルからHTTPリクエストを行います。

# JavaScriptの場合
cds watch --profile hybrid

# TypeScriptの場合
cds-ts watch --profile hybrid

注意点:

  1. ローカルマシンからSCCにHybrid Testingでアクセスする際には、BTP環境内からcallが必要となるため、VS CodeなどのIDEではなくBASを使用してください。参考リンク

  2. .envファイルには、destinationのnameとurl情報をBTP CockpitのDestinations画面から取得し、destinations変数を追記する必要があります。

destinations=[{"name":"demodestination","proxyHost":"http://127.0.0.1","proxyPort":"8887","url":"http://demodestination.dest"}]

おわりに

CAPを使用したアプリ開発のお役に立てれば幸いです。

entry.title: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News April 2025
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce our latest SAP Cloud ALM updates for April 2025 in our TOP 10 SAP Cloud ALM News blog-series! This month, we're unboxing various innovative features, specifically tailored to transform your project management, monitoring, and traceability workflows. Discover the top 10 highlights of this April release and start to boost your digital transformation journey!

 

IMPLEMENTATION UPDATES

1. Enhancements in Process Hierarchy Assignments App for Test Management

The “Process Hierarchy Assignments” app now includes new columns and tabs for test preparation and test execution, providing users with a more organized and structured view of all test-related data. With these improvements, users can easily access and manage test information, resulting in a more productive and effective test management experience.

 

2. Introducing DDA Spreadsheet Files for SAP Best Practices Scope Creation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

You can now upload a DDA (Data Driven Approach) spreadsheet file when creating a new scope for SAP Best Practices in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This functionality streamlines the scope creation process by eliminating the need for manual efforts, saving time and reducing the risk of errors. The DDA spreadsheet upload feature is also available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, providing a consistent experience across both deployment models. Furthermore, in SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the “Solution Scenario Version” dropdown list has been optimized to display only the latest version.

 

Note

  • The DDA spreadsheet file format is not identical between the private and public editions.
  • For more information, check out the “Create a Scope” in-app help topic on the “Manage Scopes” screen, or refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/manage-scopes#importing-a-process-scope-from-a-spreadsheet-create-scope-from-file- in the Help Portal.

 

 

3. New app available: Guided Implementation

The Guided Implementation app is now at your disposal, designed to provide a role-based, structured and efficient approach to task completion. Moreover, project leads are now empowered to initiate projects directly from within the app. This new feature offers a new welcome screen that guides you through focus areas and serves as a convenient gateway for project leads to access the Analytics app. Additionally, project leads can now initiate projects directly within the app, streamlining the process.

 

4. Enhanced Requirement Distribution by Release Feature

The enhanced “Requirement Distribution by Release” feature provides a structured view of requirements for each release. This feature can now be accessed through a new dropdown entry available in the Overview and Analytics applications. By utilizing this functionality, users can effectively track and analyse requirements across various releases, ultimately improving project management and planning efficiency.

 

5. Improved Timing Coordination for Defect Reporting App

The Defect Reporting app has been upgraded with new features to streamline defect reporting processes. A key improvement is the alignment of the time frame filter with the start and end dates of selected test plans. This update ensures that the chosen time frame accurately corresponds to the specific dates of your test plans. As a result, the data in your History, Created vs. Resolved, and Defect Distribution reports will be more precise and relevant to your testing activities.

 

6. Mass Upload Capability Simplifies Test Case Management

You can now save valuable time and streamline your test case management process with the mass-upload capability for manual test cases. By using a spreadsheet, you can efficiently upload multiple test cases from scratch. The app also provides a template that you can use directly, further simplifying the process. For more detailed explanations on how to use the template, refer to the Uploading Manual Test Cases on the SAP Help Portal.

 

7. Streamlined Process Assignment with Mass Edit

Boost your productivity with the enhanced “Mass Edit” feature in the “Tasks” app. You can now effortlessly assign multiple processes to project tasks, requirements, user stories, and defects in one go. This time-saving functionality simplifies your workflow, allowing you to focus on what matters most: delivering high-quality results.

 

 

OPERATIONS UPDATES

8. Advanced Name Filtering in Job & Automation Monitoring

The name filter in “Monitoring Filters and Analysis Filters” now allows you to narrow down the displayed jobs or automations by specifying the beginning or ending characters of their names. This feature utilizes the asterisk (*) symbol along with the desired characters to perform partial name matches. Here's how it works:

  1. Using * before the characters. For example: *xyz. This retrieves all jobs or automations whose names end with “xyz”.
  2. Using * after the characters. For example: efg*. This retrieves all jobs or automations whose names begin with “efg”.

This feature is valuable for quickly locating specific jobs or automations within a large list, saving time and effort in managing and monitoring workflows.

 

9. Health Monitoring now supports SAP Market Communication for Utilities

SAP Market Communication for Utilities now includes Health Monitoring capabilities. This feature provides users with access to crucial information about their tenant's certificates, such as expiration dates. By proactively monitoring and managing the health of their system, customers can ensure uninterrupted operations and timely certificate renewals.

 

10. Memory Usage Refresh Option

The “Memory Used by Messages” table in the application settings now includes an “Update” option. This feature allows users to refresh the memory consumption details for each service or system on-demand. By clicking the “Update” button, the system calculates the current memory usage and displays the information in a tabular format. To access this functionality, navigate to configuration > application settings and click on the details link next to the “Memory Used by Messages” section. For more details about this feature, please refer to the Application Settings documentation.

 

Revolutionize Your Business with SAP Cloud ALM and embrace the latest advancements in SAP Cloud ALM this April! These enhanced features are crafted to accelerate your progress and support your company's growth and innovation.

Stay connected for more transformative updates and delve into the SAP Help Portal. 

Together, we can amplify your SAP Cloud ALM experience and unlock uncharted territories of productivity and achievement. Seize the moment, and happy transforming! 🚀

entry.title: Issue with Message Popover in Standard Pick by Cart Fiori App (Extended Version)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working with the standard Fiori app – Pick by Cart, and we’ve applied some customizations/extensions on top of it. Everything had been functioning smoothly, but just recently I encountered an issue that I’m hoping someone in the community has seen before.

❗Problem:

  • The message popover icon correctly shows the number of messages, but when clicked, it doesn't display the actual message details.

🔎What I’ve Checked So Far:

  • Verified that messages are being pushed to the model

  • Checked if the MessagePopover control is initialized and visible – seems to be, but just doesn’t render the list of messages.

❓Has anyone else faced this issue?

  • Is there a known bug or behavior where the message count appears, but the popover is empty or fails to show message content?

  • Could it be related to SAPUI5 version differences, or how the MessagePopover aggregation is used (e.g., MessageItem vs. deprecated types)? Control being used here is MessagePopoverItem

🛠️ Any resolutions or workarounds you’ve used would be really helpful!

Thanks in advance for any pointers! Happy to provide code snippets or version details if that helps narrow it down.

App ID : F2793

entry.title: SAP Business One Mobile Service Traffic Abnormality
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We use the version of BOne for Windows
SAP Business One 10.0 (10.00.240) SP 2402 (Security) HOTFIX1 (64-bit)

System Layout Description
1. We set up Nginx-Server on Azure VM and used VPN to connect to AP server and DB Server in the office to exchange data.
2. Users use BOne APP through the Internet.
3. For security reasons, we set up the B1IF service on Azure-Nginx.
4. Nginx will connect to the SAP DB Server through VPN and the office internal network to exchange data.

Question:
During the two years we used the SAP BOne APP mobile service, the average daily traffic generated was less than 100KBs.
Starting from 2025/02/13, the Internet traffic was abnormally high, reaching 20MBs per day, which lasted for 2 weeks.
We know from the firewall log that Azure-Nginx has been exchanging data with the DB SERVER during this period, but I don’t know what exactly B1IF is doing with SAP DB?
I want to try to start from the SQL DB perspective and find out if there is a log that records the exchanges during this period, so as to parse out what actions are continuously generating traffic, but I don't know which database table or related technical documents or support services to look for?

This incident directly caused extremely high costs and losses to the company, so we are eager to know the cause and establish countermeasures.

We need help or advice to prevent further harm in the future.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Simplifying My Home Activation from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 or higher
entry.content:
Innovation made easier!  Enabling My Home for SAP S/4HANA has been streamlined from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Save time and effort by using the settings in task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4.

In our previous blog, "SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Empowering Your Homepage: Enabling My Home for SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01", we explored the detailed steps required to enable My Home in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. We walked through the structure, technical components, configuration steps, and necessary authorizations to activate My Home manually.

IMPORTANT : If you are looking to understand the structure of My Home i.e. its main components”, or its building blocks and technical components, please refer to our previous blog.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release, the process of enabling My Home has been further streamlined. As a part of this blog, we will focus on the enhancements introduced in FPS02, where Steps 1 through Step 4 described in the previous blog are now automated via the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task list. This significantly reduces manual effort, making My Home activation quicker and more efficient.

Comparison of SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 Task List Across SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, and FPS02

 

Simplified My Home Activation with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02

 

Automation via the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 Task List

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 release onwards, Steps 1 through 4 explained in the pervious blog are now automated as part of the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task list. This task list consolidates these key setup steps into a single run, simplifying and streamlining the activation process.

Note:If you have already activated SAP Fiori foundation on your SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 using Rapid Activation and executed the SAP Fiori - Initial Setup for Fiori Application S/4 (SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4) task list, the following tasks are automatically included as part of the default task list run, and no additional manual execution is required.

However, if you have upgraded from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (or lower releases of SAP S/4HANA) to SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, you must re-execute the following four tasks (from the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task list ) described in this blog to ensure My Home is fully enabled with the latest updates.

 

Step (described in the previous blog)Corresponding task from the SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 task listAutomation Details
1 - Enabling Spaces and Pages LayoutActivate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)Configures FLP property SPACES with system setting checks.
2 - Activating the My Home UI5 ComponentActivate HTTP Services FoundationActivates the SAPUI5 Component ux.eng.s4producthomes1 (SICF node: BSP PRODUCT_HOMES1).
3 - Activating the Required OData V2 and V4 ServicesActivate Gateway OData Services Foundation (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE) & Publish Service Groups Foundation (/IWFND/V4_ADMIN)Publishes the following service groups:
- /IWNGW/NOTIFICATION (Notification)
- /UI2/INSIGHTS_SRV (Insight Cards)
Note: TASKPROCESSING (My Inbox) and C_SITNMYSITUATION_CDS (Situation Handling) are NOT included in this automation and must be activated manually if required.
Please refer to Step 4 from the previous blog for manual activation steps.
4 - Configuring Launchpad ParametersActivate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)Configures all necessary FLP parameters for My Home page, including enablement of the page, Custom Component, and UI5 Insights.

 

Step 1 through Step 4 can be executed cumulatively on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 (or higher)

  1. Navigate to the transaction STC01 (Task Manager for Technical Configuration).
    STC01 : Task Manager for Technical Configuration
  2. Enter the Task List name as SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 (which is used to activate Fiori foundation) and click on the Generate Task List Run (F8) button.

  3. On the next screen with generated task list, uncheck all the tasks using Skip All Tasks (F7) button and then only Select the task Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF). After selecting the task, You will get an Information popup that the activation step has dependencies on other steps like creating transports, activating services etc., click Continue for all the dependent steps.

    Skip all tasks for SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 and select 'Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)' task

     

    By clicking on the Change Parameters button for this task, you could configure the following Fiori Launchpad Parameters required for My Home enablement, which have been highlighted already as a part of the blog. For now, you could just leave them as-is to default and continue.

    Change Parameters for the task 'Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)'
  4. Additionally, also select the task Publish Service Groups Foundation (/IWFND/V4_ADMIN) which would publish the /IWNGW/NOTIFICATION (Notification) and /UI2/INSIGHTS_SRV (Insight Cards) OData V4 services.

    After selecting this task, only the following 5 task should remain selected.
    • Create / Select Customizing Request (SE09)
    • Activate and Configure FLP for Spaces and Pages (/UI2/FLP_CUS_CONF)
    • Activate Gateway OData Services Foundation (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE)
    • Publish Service Groups Foundation (/IWFND/V4_ADMIN)
    • Activate HTTP services Foundation
  5. Click on the Start/Resume Task List Run in the Dialog button to start the task list run.

IMPORTANT: As mentioned above, the task list SAP_FIORI_FOUNDATION_S4 available with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 would not activate the optional functionality like My Inbox and Situation Handling related OData services. Please follow the references mentioned in the original blog should you be interested to activate them.

Step 5: Assigning Relevant Authorizations via a PFCG Role

 

The role creation and authorization setup for My Home in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 remains the same as FPS01. You can follow the same steps described in the previous blog to create and configure the required PFCG role.

For a quick summary, here are the key steps:

  1. Create a PFCG security role (e.g., Z_SR_MY_HOME)

    • Navigate to transaction PFCG, enter a role name, and click Create Single Role.
    • Provide a role description and switch to the Menu tab.

  2. Add the Required OData Services

    • TASKPROCESSING (for My Inbox)
    • C_SITNMYSITUATION_CDS (for Situations)
    • INTEROP (for personalization, should already be active)
    • /UI2/INSIGHTS_CARDS_READ_SRV (for Insights Cards)

  3. Assign Target Mappings for My Inbox and My Situations

    • Use SAP Fiori Launchpad Content Manager (UI2/FLPCM_CUST).
    • Create a Launchpad Catalog (Z_BC_MY_HOME).
    • Add Target Mappings for My Inbox (F0862) and My Situations (F4154).

  4. Assign the Role to Users

    • Open the Role Maintenance transaction PFCG, go to the User tab, and assign the role to business users.

Note: Ensure that users have business roles containing spaces and pages, so My Home functions correctly.

Note :  

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Extend S/4HANA Material Master with Custom Fields and Transfer to Decentralized EWM Product Master
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm working with SAP S/4HANA and a decentralized SAP EWM system. My requirement is to add custom fields to the material master in S/4HANA (e.g., MARA or MARC level), and ensure that these custom fields are transferred to the product master in decentralized EWM.

So far, I understand that:

  1. We can use Custom Fields and Logic (SCFD) or append structures to extend the material master in S/4HANA.

  2. The material master is sent to EWM via DRF (Data Replication Framework).

My questions are:

  • What is the recommended approach to ensure that the custom fields are included in the replication from S/4HANA to decentralized EWM?

  • Do we need to enhance the DRF or is there a BAdI or enhancement in DRF that supports transferring these custom fields?

  • On the EWM side, how can we ensure that the custom fields are visible in the product master ?

Any best practices, sample BAdIs, or links to SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: API to POST to SAP | RAP | SEGW | if_http_extension~handle_request
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have a requirement for a non-SAP cloud system to POST a simple JSON paylod to SAP S/4 and the schema is something similar to

{
	"event": "initiated",
	"date": "2021-10-12T19:26:01Z",
	"data": {
		"id": "MMM12345678",
		"amount": "100",
		"currency": "GBP",
		"payment_method": {
			"type": "card"
		},
		"fields": {
			"reference": "REF-1234",
			"id": "123456789"
		},
		"payer": {
			"first_name": "TEST",
			"last_name": "TESTL",
			"email": "Test.Test@Test.com"
		}
	}
}

No GET needed. What is the best way to handle this? REST handler class or an ODATA  service using SEGW or ODATA service using RAP. If RAP is the reommended way, how to define the nested json structure as custom entity for the above payload please?

Thank you.

Sathya

entry.title: SAP CPQ User Report
entry.content:
Hello SAP CPQ Community,

We are looking for a way to generate a report in SAP CPQ that provides a list of all active users, along with the date their account was created and the date of their last login.

Our goal is to effectively manage our user licenses and identify users who may no longer need access based on their inactivity. Comparing the 'creation date' and 'last login date' will be crucial for this analysis.

Specifically, we are interested in knowing:

Is there a standard report available in SAP CPQ that includes this information? If so, what is the name of the report and how can it be accessed?
If a standard report doesn't exist, what are the possible methods to extract this data? For example, are there specific APIs, data tables, or reporting tools within SAP CPQ that we can leverage?
Could a custom report be created to achieve this? If so, what would be the general steps or considerations for building such a report?
Are there any best practices or recommended approaches within SAP CPQ for monitoring user activity and managing licenses effectively?
We are using 2503 SAP CPQ version, Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your time and assistance."

entry.title: Re: How to install and configure the RealCore CPI dashboard
entry.content:
Hi @r_herrmann ,
Thanks for the detailed blog!

I’ve followed the steps you mentioned, but I'm running into an issue when calling the endpoint /itspaces/odata/1.0/workspace.svc/ContentEntities.ContentPackages. I'm getting a "login callback" error.

In my setup, I'm using the client ID and client secret from the BTP → SAP Process Integration Runtime → integration-flow → Service Key. However, the parameter Credential Name/SAP_CPI_AUTH_API_CREDENTIALS_BASICAUTH seems to expect a different kind of credential (a user/password pair).

entry.title: supplier invoice foreign currency
entry.content:
Where can the exchange rate be updated so that the valuation of an invoice recorded in foreign currency is registered in our company currency according to the supplier's invoice valuation?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: supplier invoice foreign currency
entry.content:
Where to update the exchange rate:

1. Supplier Invoice Document Itself
When manually creating or editing a supplier invoice, you can manually override the exchange rate in the “Valuation” tab or “Prices and Taxes” tab (depending on the UI).

This sets the specific exchange rate for that invoice, ensuring your company currency valuation reflects the supplier's invoice valuation.

2. Exchange Rate Table (General Ledger Work Center)
Path: General Ledger → Master Data → Exchange Rates

You can update or maintain daily or periodic exchange rates for currency pairs .

These rates are automatically used if no manual override is done during invoice posting.

3. Business Configuration (for Source of Exchange Rate)
You can configure which source of exchange rate should be prioritized .

Path: Business Configuration → Overview → Search “Exchange Rate Type”

4. Invoice via Purchase Order
If the invoice is created via a PO, and no exchange rate is entered in the invoice, the system uses the exchange rate valid on the invoice document date from the exchange rate table.

Kindly have a look into the below help doc: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2c2cb764722d101483aca5ac7b49067b.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Handover of test cases to test from one user to another user SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear,

The creation of a test case and link to a test plan and execute is clear, only to have in really practice is different, hopefully you will able to give us more clearity as we are going to use SAP Cloud ALM for each project. We are going to start with the test execution and have created multiple test cases, we have now questions regrading test execution and test plans. Any suggestion are welcome:

  1. When creating a test case we have the possibility to create a test plan and link the test case to these test plan, but when linking multiple test case to a test plan we cannot define the order/sequence of the test cases.
  2. We have only the possibility to add one tester in the test plan? What if I have more testers to test on one teste case ?
  3. If we assign a tester does he receive any e-mail? 
  4. Is there any e-mail chain/notification regarding test management except defects?
  5. How can we handover a certain test case/execution to another (does he also receive an e-mail) ?  

Thank you in advance and kind regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Handover of test cases to test from one user to anoth...
entry.content:
Manual Test Cases are not designed to have a sequence of execution between each other. There is no enforced execution sequence dependency. Manual Test Cases can be executed in any order. 

If you need to enforce a sequence dependency, then you can write the first step of a Test Case so that it outlines that another Test Case must first be completed as a prerequisite. Again, SAP Cloud ALM Test Execution cannot enforce this, your users will need to abide by the steps.

The display order/sequence of the manual Test Cases is only visual in the Test Preparation and Test Execution apps. It is not intended as an order of execution. The display order can be changed by clicking the "Sort" icon in the result table (two vertical arrows, one pointing down, the other pointing up). Multiple field values are available as sort options.

Test Cases aren't assigned to individual users, Test Plans are. In Test Planning, the "responsible" person is the tester. The search field "Tester" in the Test Execution app will be the same value as the assigned "responsible" person for the Test Plan. It is not currently possible to assign multiple users as being responsible for a Test Plan, only a single user can be assigned. If you need to change the tester for a Test Plan (and all its Test Cases), you must change the "responsible" person for the Test Plan.

KBA 3390449 outlines the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation use cases that support notifications (including e-mails). Currently Test Management functionality is not included in this list.

If you wish to influence the future development of SAP Cloud ALM and provide direction to our Developers in relation to any of these topics, please log a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Customer Influence campaign.

entry.title: How to check if expected data are released from Universal Journals to Group reporting(FIN-CS-COR-DT)
entry.content:
1) check if GRPL is activated (t-cd: CXB3)
1.1) if GRPL is activated, then please check in t-cd: FINCS_MAP_RLDNR_R,  get the Ledger which used for GRPL.
1.2) If GRPL is not activated, please check field value TF200-RLDNR_SOURCE to get the Ledger which used for data source.

2) Check in table FINCS_BUNIT , T001.  From table FINCS_BUNIT, you can get the assigned company for the cons.unit. And then use it to check in table T001 with RCOMP = company, with this you will know the company code(BUKRS)
3) With  ledger, company code, you may go to t-cd: FAGLB03, to check the balance for specific account in each month. Example below:


4) On the other hands, you may go to the data monitor, and run data release in test mode for specific cons.unit and fiscal year/period.  From the results screen, you can check the the account balance for by click on the toggle button. Please check the picture below:


5) Another approach to check the account balance is the app "Group Data analysis".
Input value for filter "Cons.unit" and "Document type = 0F", and in the result list, add "Account number".


6) If you found that the balance in FAGLB03 does not corresponding to the results in Group data analysis. (in my example, the balance is -2000 in FAGLB03 for 12530000 , but in GDA, the balance is -1000 in FAGLB03 for 12530000), then you will need to do data analysis in order to find the reason for the difference.
In that case, please refer to my blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-check-the-reason-when-some-items-are-not-released-from-acdoca-to/ba-p/14088012

entry.title: Switch from SAP_COM_0086 (Neo) to SAP_COM_0623 (Cloud Foundry)
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will guide you through the process of switching from the theming communication scenario SAP_COM_0086 (SAP BTP, Neo environment) to SAP_COM_0623 (SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment). This switch enables you to access the latest features of the UI theme designer, such as theme migration.

Background

In an S/4HANA Cloud system, you use the UI theme designer from either the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment or the SAP BTP, Neo environment to create and manage custom themes for SAP applications. To do this, you must implement one of the communication scenarios for the UI theme designer. For more details, refer to the documentation on Integrating UI Theme Designer.

Why is it important to switch to the communication scenario  SAP_COM_0623 (SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment)?

  • Sunsetting of SAP BTP, Neo Environment: The SAP BTP, Neo environment will be retired by the end of 2028. For more information, refer to the blog post Farewell, Neo!
  • End-of-Life for Belize Theme: The Belize theme has reached its end-of-life with SAPUI5 1.135. By using the UI theme designer on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, you can migrate your custom themes to modern themes based on Quartz or Horizon. For further details, see:
    • SAP note 3595596.
    • Blog posts: Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori and Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal.

Prerequisites

  • You already have a subaccount on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.

Procedure

Step 1: Initial Setup

  1. Set up the UI theme designer on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment as described in the guide Initial Setup of UI Theme Designer: Portal Scenario.

Step 2: Copy Themes

  1. Export your custom themes from the UI theme designer in the SAP BTP, Neo environment. For detailed instructions, refer to Exporting Themes.
  2. Import your custom themes into the UI theme designer in the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment. For detailed instructions, refer to Importing Themes.
  3. If your custom theme is based on the Belize theme, migrate it to a theme based on Horizon or Quartz. See Migrating Themes for further information.
    Note: The Belize theme has been deprecated since SAPUI5 version 1.120 and was removed in SAPUI5 version 1.135.
  4. After a successful migration, rename your theme accordingly. Use the same theme ID as before to ensure that no changes are needed for users to continue using the theme. For detailed instructions, refer to Renaming Themes.
  5. Test your theme using the preview pages available in the UI theme designer. For detailed instructions, refer to Editing Themes.
    Note: Migration will keep as many of your changes as possible, such as changed parameters or uploaded resources like a logo. However, there will be cases where the result is not 100% correct. Revisit the migration result and use it as a starting point for manual fine-tuning.
  6. Publish your theme to make it available for use. For detailed instructions, refer to Publishing Themes.

Step 3: Switch Communication Scenario

Important: Perform this step during a maintenance window when few or no users are active in the system. While switching the communication scenario, applications will temporarily display the SAP standard theme.

  1. Delete the communication scenario SAP_COM_0086 for the SAP BTP, Neo environment.
  2. Create a new communication scenario for SAP_COM_0623 (SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment) as described in the guide SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Environment (SAP_COM_0623).
  3. Test your custom themes with the SAP Fiori Launchpad in the S/4HANA Cloud system.

Recap

By following the outlined steps—setting up the UI theme designer on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, migrating your themes, and switching the communication scenario—you can modernize your theming approach and ensure a seamless transition with minimal disruption to your users.

For additional information, refer to the linked SAP Notes and guides. If you need further assistance, feel free to reach out via component CA-UI2-THD.

root:-

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Event Root'
@ObjectModel.compositionRoot: true
define view entity ZI_Event

{
  key id    as EventId,

      _Payer
}

entry.title: Upload/Create Logistics Area in Bulk in Business ByDesign.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are looking for standard upload function in ByD where we can upload/create bin locations in bulk against the location as the number of count for bin location against each location is high  and we have multiple locations,

Please let us know if we can upload/create bin location via excel template in ByD.

Regards,
Vimal ,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Upload/Create Logistics Area in Bulk in Business ByDe...
entry.content:
Dear Vimal, 

There is no option to perform mass upload of logistics areas via Excel. However, you can create multiple logistics areas via the below given steps in the system.

  1. Go to Supply Chain Design Master Data
  2. Navigate to the Locations view.
  3. Search and select the relevant Site.
  4. Choose the Edit Site Layout option.
  5. Select the Add Multiple Logistics Areas button.
  6. Enter the Pattern ID, From and To Logistics Area IDs and Reference Logistics area from which the new logistics areas/bins should be created.
  7. Choose the OK button.
  8. Review the list of Logistics Areas created and choose the Save button.

Note: Choose the Logistics Area for reference from under the same site.

Regards, 
Anand
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Connectivity Tests Before Executing Hybrid Testing with CAP: Connecting to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise
entry.content:
Introduction

In this article, I will explain "Hybrid Testing" in the context of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), which allows development while accessing real data from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise. I will focus on connectivity tests that are useful before executing Hybrid Testing. For detailed information on setting up the environment, please refer to the links below.

  • SAP Gateway - Activate and Maintain Services
  • Discovery Center - Connect S/4HANA system using Cloud Connector
  • SAP CAP  - Consuming Services #Connect to Remote Services Locally
  • SAP CAP - Hybrid Testing
  • SAP Cloud SKD - Connecting to External Systems From the Business Application Studio #Connection to a...

What is Hybrid Testing?

In app development using CAP, testing is typically conducted in a local environment using mock data. However, there are cases where you need to access cloud or on-premise services. "Hybrid Testing" is a useful method in such cases. By using this testing method, developers can run applications in a local environment while connecting to remote services, thereby improving development efficiency.

Challenges of Hybrid Testing with SAP S/4HANA On-Premise

While there are advantages, accessing on-premise systems relies on services such as SAP Destination Service, SAP Connectivity Service, and Cloud Connector. This makes the environment setup more complex compared to local testing with mock data. Therefore, I introduce connectivity tests that can help streamline the environment setup process.

Connectivity Tests

The connectivity tests introduced are in the order shown in the diagram below. I recommend testing them sequentially, starting with those with fewer dependencies.

1. Connectivity Test from SAP Gateway Client

The SAP Gateway Client, with T-code /IWFND/GW_CLIENT, functions as an HTTP(S) client to test OData services. After entering the appropriate HTTP method, OData query (format, top...), request URI, request headers like Content-Type, and payload, click "Execute" on the app to check the availability and response of the target OData service within S/4HANA.

If an error occurs, verify that the OData service is activated and that the correct path is specified, among other potential issues.

2. Connectivity Test from Cloud Connector Administration UI

After a successful test from the Gateway Client, test the connectivity from SCC to the on-premise system. Specifically, click the leftmost icon in Actions to verify the connection from the Virtual host defined in SCC to the Internal host (on-premise). If 'Reachable' is displayed, the test is successful.

3. Connectivity Test from BTP Cockpit

In the BTP Cockpit, perform a "Check Connection" to SCC to verify if the URL of the Destination can be reached. Note that this test does not guarantee that the Virtual host of the Destination Service (SCC) is operational, but only confirms reachability.

'The check does not guarantee that the target system or service is operational. It only verifies if ...

If an error occurs, there may be issues with the SCC settings, version, or server availability.

4. Connectivity Test from SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

Finally, perform Hybrid Testing itself. Bind the destination and auth services to the app deployed in the Cloud Foundry Runtime, and execute the following command to make an HTTP request from the HTTP file.

# JavaScript
cds watch --profile hybrid

# TypeScript
cds-ts watch --profile hybrid

Notes:

  1. When accessing SCC from a local machine using Hybrid Testing, it is necessary to make calls from within the BTP environment. Therefore, please use BAS instead of IDEs like VS Code. Reference link
  2. In the .env file, you need to add the destinations variable by obtaining the name and URL information of the destination from the Destinations page in the BTP Cockpit.
destinations=[{"name":"demodestination","proxyHost":"http://127.0.0.1","proxyPort":"8887","url":"http://demodestination.dest"}]

Conclusion

I hope this helps you in your app development using CAP.
